docs.title: RentalBasic Documentation
docs.text: RentalBasic is a marketplace where customers can book event equipment and rental providers can manage their listings, bookings, and payouts.

docs.title: Who This Is For
docs.text:
CustomersRental Providers
  • Getting Started
  • Customer Guide
  • Refunds and Cancellations
  • Terms of Service
  • Getting Started
  • Rental Partner Guide
  • Rental Partner Payouts
  • Terms of Service

docs.title: What You Can Expect
docs.text:
• A clear booking flow from browsing to delivery confirmation
  • Transparent cancellation and refund rules
  • Practical guidance for rental partners on operations and payouts

Confidential Information

This public documentation intentionally omits internal implementation details and security-sensitive configuration.

docs.title: Terms of Service
docs.text:
Last updated: March 15, 2026

This document is a production-ready draft for the chair-rental platform. Replace all bracketed placeholders before publishing.

docs.title: 1. Operator
docs.text:
This platform is operated by Kim Cyriel Avillanosa, an individual / sole proprietor doing business as rentalbasic (the "Platform", "we", "us", or "our").

Business address: 258-A Manalo Extension, Bgy Milagrosa, Puerto Princesa City, Palawan, Philippines

Support email: careers.kmavillanosa@gmail.com

Support phone: 09452873791

docs.title: 2. Scope of Service
docs.text:
The Platform is an online marketplace that connects customers seeking event and rental equipment with independent vendors who list chairs, tables, tents, audiovisual equipment, decor, and related items.

Unless expressly stated otherwise in writing, the Platform is not the owner, manufacturer, warehouse operator, transporter, or insurer of the listed items. Vendors remain responsible for the equipment they list and the services they provide.

docs.title: 3. Acceptance of Terms
docs.text:
By creating an account, accessing the Platform, listing inventory, placing a booking request, or otherwise using the Platform, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service, the Vendor Agreement where applicable, the Platform Commission Policy, and the Liability Disclaimer.

If you do not agree, do not use the Platform.

docs.title: 4. Eligibility and Accounts
docs.text:
You must be at least 18 years old and legally capable of entering into binding contracts.

You agree to:

  • provide accurate and current account information;
  • maintain the confidentiality of your login credentials;
  • use the Platform only for lawful purposes; and
  • promptly update any material changes to your identity, contact information, or payment details.

You are responsible for all activity under your account unless the activity results directly from our gross negligence or willful misconduct.

docs.title: 5. Marketplace Role
docs.text:
The Platform provides technology, discovery, booking, payment facilitation, and related administrative tools. We do not guarantee that any booking request will be accepted, fulfilled, completed without issue, or suited to a particular event.

Each rental transaction is ultimately between the customer and the vendor. The Platform may facilitate payments, communications, and dispute handling, but does not become a party to the underlying rental contract except to the limited extent needed to collect fees, enforce platform rules, or process refunds, payouts, or chargebacks.

docs.title: 6. Bookings, Payments, and Cancellations
docs.text:
Booking totals may include item charges, delivery charges, service charges, taxes if applicable, and other disclosed amounts.

Where checkout is enabled, payments may be processed through third-party providers such as PayMongo. By completing payment, you also agree to the applicable processor terms.

Confirmed bookings, cancellation timing, and any refund amount are governed by the booking details shown at checkout, the applicable cancellation settings, and any mandatory rules under applicable law.

We may place a booking on hold, delay payout, reverse settlement, or suspend an account when reasonably necessary to investigate fraud, chargebacks, abuse, duplicate transactions, legal claims, or safety concerns.

docs.title: 7. User Responsibilities
docs.text:
You agree not to:

  • submit false, misleading, or fraudulent information;
  • post unlawful, infringing, abusive, or defamatory content;
  • manipulate pricing, ratings, or booking status;
  • bypass the Platform to avoid platform fees for leads or transactions originating through the Platform, except where we expressly allow it in writing;
  • interfere with system security, uptime, APIs, or other users; or
  • use the Platform in violation of applicable law, licensing requirements, or third-party rights.

docs.title: 8. Vendor-Specific Terms
docs.text: If you act as a vendor, you also agree to the Vendor Agreement. In case of conflict between these Terms of Service and the Vendor Agreement for vendor matters, the Vendor Agreement controls.

docs.title: 9. Intellectual Property
docs.text:
The Platform software, branding, workflows, interface elements, text, logos, and non-user content are owned by or licensed to us and are protected by intellectual property laws.

You retain ownership of content you submit, but you grant us a non-exclusive, worldwide, royalty-free license to host, store, reproduce, adapt, publish, and display that content as needed to operate, market, secure, and improve the Platform.

docs.title: 10. Suspension and Termination
docs.text:
We may suspend, restrict, or terminate access to the Platform, remove listings, cancel bookings not yet fully performed, or hold payouts when we reasonably believe it is necessary to:

  • enforce these terms or platform policies;
  • protect customers, vendors, or the public;
  • investigate fraud, abuse, or legal non-compliance; or
  • comply with a law, court order, payment-provider requirement, or governmental request.

You may stop using the Platform at any time, but accrued obligations, unresolved disputes, payment liabilities, indemnities, and surviving clauses remain effective.

docs.title: 11. Disclaimers
docs.text:
The Platform is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis. To the maximum extent permitted by law, we disclaim all implied warranties, including merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, non-infringement, uninterrupted availability, and error-free operation.

We do not warrant the conduct, quality, legality, safety, delivery performance, inventory condition, business permits, insurance coverage, or truthfulness of any vendor, customer, listing, review, or third-party service.

docs.title: 12. Limitation of Liability
docs.text:
To the maximum extent permitted by law, we will not be liable for indirect, incidental, special, consequential, exemplary, or punitive damages, including lost profits, lost bookings, lost goodwill, event disruption, reputational harm, or data loss.

Our aggregate liability arising out of or related to the Platform will not exceed the greater of:

  • the total platform fees actually received by us from the specific booking giving rise to the claim; or
  • [TO BE FINALIZED LIABILITY CAP].

This limitation applies whether the claim is based on contract, tort, statute, strict liability, or any other theory, even if we were advised of the possibility of such damages.

Nothing in these terms excludes liability that cannot legally be excluded under applicable law.

docs.title: 13. Indemnity
docs.text:
You agree to defend, indemnify, and hold harmless Kim Cyriel Avillanosa, the Platform, and their agents, contractors, and service providers from claims, liabilities, damages, losses, costs, and expenses, including reasonable legal fees, arising out of:

  • your use of the Platform;
  • your listings, content, equipment, event operations, or conduct;
  • your breach of these terms or other platform policies; or
  • your violation of law or third-party rights.

docs.title: 14. Privacy and Third-Party Services
docs.text:
The Platform may rely on third-party services for authentication, mapping, geocoding, hosting, messaging, analytics, and payments. Your use of those integrations may also be subject to the third party's terms and privacy practices.

You acknowledge that external services may become unavailable, change pricing, rate-limit requests, or suspend access without prior notice from us.

docs.title: 15. Changes to Terms
docs.text:
We may update these terms from time to time. Material changes may be posted on the Platform, emailed, or otherwise made available through reasonable notice.

Your continued use of the Platform after the effective date of the updated terms constitutes acceptance of the revised terms.

docs.title: 16. Governing Law and Dispute Venue
docs.text:
These terms are governed by the laws of Philippines, without regard to conflict-of-law rules.

Any dispute arising from or related to these terms or the Platform will be brought exclusively before the courts located in Puerto Princesa City, Palawan, Philippines, unless applicable law requires another venue.

docs.title: 17. Contact
docs.text:
For legal notices, billing issues, or policy questions, contact:

Kim Cyriel Avillanosa

258-A Manalo Extension, Bgy Milagrosa, Puerto Princesa City, Palawan, Philippines

careers.kmavillanosa@gmail.com

09452873791

docs.title: Customer Guide
docs.text: This guide explains the full customer journey from discovery to completion.

docs.title: 1. Find Rental Partners and Equipment
docs.text:
• Use the map and search to discover nearby rental providers.
  • Open a rental partner storefront to view items, rates, and reviews.

docs.title: 2. Place a Booking
docs.text:
Booking has four steps:

  1. Select items and quantities.
  2. Choose rental dates.
  3. Set delivery location.
  4. Review total and proceed to payment.

docs.title: 3. Pay Securely
docs.text:
Available payment methods include e-wallets and cards.

Rental partners may choose: - Full payment at checkout - Downpayment first, with remaining balance before delivery

docs.title: 4. Track Booking Status
docs.text: Status Meaning UNPAID Booking created, no payment yet CHECKOUT_PENDING Payment is being processed DOWNPAYMENT_PAID First payment complete (downpayment mode) HELD Payment is secured CONFIRMED Rental partner accepted the booking IN_DELIVERY Items are on the way DELIVERED Rental partner marked delivery complete COMPLETED You confirmed delivery CANCELLED Booking was cancelled REFUNDED Refund was processed DISPUTED Booking is under dispute review

docs.title: 5. Confirm Delivery
docs.text: Only confirm delivery after receiving the items in acceptable condition.

docs.title: 6. Leave a Review
docs.text: After completion, submit a rating and optional comment.

docs.title: 7. Cancel and Get Refunds
docs.text:
Cancellation refund depends on timing before the rental start:

Timing Refund Before rental partner confirmation Full refund More than 48 hours before start Full refund 24 to 48 hours before start Partial refund Less than 24 hours before start No refund

docs.title: 8. Raise a Dispute
docs.text: If there is a serious issue, open a dispute from your booking details and provide evidence when requested.

docs.title: 9. Chat and Notifications
docs.text: Use in-app chat for coordination. Enable notifications for real-time updates.

docs.title: Sign In
docs.text:
RentalBasic uses Google sign-in.

  1. Open the app.
  2. Click Sign in with Google.
  3. Complete the Google consent screen.
  4. Return to RentalBasic and continue.

docs.title: Account Roles
docs.text: Role How You Get It What You Can Do Customer Automatic on first sign-in Browse vendors, place bookings, manage orders Rental Partner Apply and pass verification Manage shop, inventory, bookings, and payouts Admin Granted by platform owner Manage platform operations

docs.title: Which App to Use
docs.text: App Who It Is For Customer App Customers booking rentals Staff App Rental partners and admins

docs.title: Become a Rental Partner
docs.text:
1. Sign in as a customer.
  2. Open Become a Rental Partner.
  3. Submit required business and identity details.
  4. Wait for review and approval.

docs.title: Notifications
docs.text: Allow browser notifications to receive booking, payment, delivery, and dispute updates.

docs.title: Rental Partner Guide
docs.text: This guide covers rental partner onboarding, daily operations, and payouts.

docs.title: 1. Apply as a Rental Partner
docs.text: Prepare required identity and business documents, submit your application, and wait for review.

docs.title: 2. Set Up Your Shop
docs.text:
• Configure shop name, description, and service area
  • Add clear item photos and complete listing details

docs.title: 3. Manage Inventory
docs.text:
• Add items, quantities, rates, and variants
  • Keep stock updated to avoid failed bookings

docs.title: 4. Configure Pricing
docs.text: Set delivery rates and additional service fees where applicable.

docs.title: 5. Manage Bookings
docs.text: Typical flow: - Booking received - Confirm booking - Deliver items - Upload delivery proof - Customer confirms delivery

docs.title: 6. Payment Modes
docs.text: You can require: - Full payment - Downpayment plus remaining balance

docs.title: 7. Payouts
docs.text:
Payout records move through these stages:

Status Meaning HELD Funds are temporarily held READY Eligible for release PAID Released

New rental partners may have a short hold period on early orders.

docs.title: 8. Disputes and Compliance
docs.text: Respond promptly to dispute requests and submit clear evidence. Repeated policy violations can lead to warnings or suspension.

docs.title: How Payments Work
docs.text: This page explains payment behavior at a business level.

docs.title: Payment Options
docs.text: Rental partners can choose between: - Full payment - Downpayment with later balance payment

docs.title: Typical Customer Payment Journey
docs.text:
sequenceDiagram
    participant Customer
    participant App
    participant PaymentProvider as Secure Payment Provider
    participant RentalPartner as Rental Partner

    Customer->>App: Place booking and continue to payment
    App->>PaymentProvider: Start secure checkout
    PaymentProvider-->>App: Payment success
    App-->>RentalPartner: Booking is ready for confirmation
    RentalPartner-->>Customer: Confirms booking and prepares delivery

docs.title: Important Notes
docs.text:
• Customer funds remain protected until delivery is confirmed.
  • Payment progress is visible in booking status.
  • If downpayment is enabled, the remaining balance must be completed before delivery.

docs.title: Rental Partner Payouts
docs.text: This page explains when rental partner earnings are released.

docs.title: Payout Lifecycle
docs.text:
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> HELD
    HELD --> READY
    READY --> PAID

docs.title: What Each Status Means
docs.text: Status Meaning HELD Funds are secured and waiting for release eligibility READY Payout can be released PAID Payout has been released

docs.title: New Rental Partner Hold
docs.text: For platform safety, early rental partner orders may have a short hold period before release.

docs.title: Cancellation Policy
docs.text: Timing Refund Cancelled before rental partner confirms Full refund Cancelled more than 48 hours before start Full refund Cancelled 24 to 48 hours before start Partial refund Cancelled less than 24 hours before start No refund

docs.title: Refund Processing
docs.text:
• Refunds are sent back to the original payment method.
  • Processing speed depends on the payment provider and bank or wallet timelines.

docs.title: Disputes
docs.text: If cancellation or delivery issues are contested, admin review is used to determine final resolution.
